stem: A digital marketing study surveys 25 users shown the ad and observes 8 positive outcomes out of 25 (32.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (13 observations, rate = 23.1%) and Group B (12 observations, rate = 41.7%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 34% and 50% of the population. Estimate the true click-through rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.32. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.3421 (range [0.3278, 0.3563]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.172, 0.5159]. Point estimate: 0.3421.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.42: 0.42×0.239 + 0.58×0.4167 = 0.3421.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
